ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Designs and implements efficient irrigation systems for agricultural purposes, focusing on water conservation and optimal crop yield.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: An Irrigation Engineer/Micro-irrigation Specialist is responsible for planning, designing, and executing irrigation systems for agricultural fields, taking into account soil type, crop requirements, and water availability. They must have a deep understanding of hydrology, hydraulics, and crop water requirements to ensure optimal water use and minimize waste.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.Tech in Agricultural Engineering or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in pursuing a career in irrigation engineering can start by taking courses in mathematics, physics, and chemistry.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can bridge their skills by taking courses in hydrology, hydraulics, and irrigation engineering.

future_builder.description: Experienced irrigation engineers can advance their careers by taking courses in advanced irrigation technologies, such as precision irrigation and drip irrigation.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: Minimum 2 years of experience as an Irrigation Engineer

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can switch to irrigation engineering by acquiring relevant skills and experience.
survival_skills_needed: Programming skills in languages like Python or R
portfolio_proof_required: Project reports on water conservation and irrigation management

roadmap.title: Capstone & Portfolio
topics: Design and implement an irrigation system for a real-world project
Present a portfolio of irrigation engineering projects
resources_keywords: GitHub repository for irrigation engineering projects
